name: Medellín Castle Loop from Don Benito
name_original: Castillo de Medellín — circular desde Don Benito
description: This moderate 43.0-mile (69.3 km) road cycling route from Don Benito takes you to the historic Castillo de Medellín with great views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Don Benito traverse the diverse landscapes of Extremadura, Spain. The region features scenic rural areas, connecting towns and historical sites. Cyclists can explore the Sierra de Ortiga, characterized by cork oaks, rockroses, and granite domes, or ride around the Embalse de Orellana. The terrain primarily consists of paved surfaces, offering options for various skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Don Benito The most popular road cycling route is Don Benito - Villanueva de la Serena — circular by the population of La Haba, an easy 25.4 miles (40.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete, passing through rural Extremaduran landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Medellín Castle Loop from Don Benito, a moderate 43.0 miles (69.3 km) path. This route offers views of historical sites like Medellín Castle amidst the rural scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Don Benito – La Haba loop from Don Benito, a 51.6 miles (83.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Don Benito is defined by rural Extremaduran landscapes, the Sierra de Ortiga with its cork oaks and granite domes, and routes around the Embalse de Orellana. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Don Benito's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Don Benito?
A: Don Benito offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 20 routes tracked on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Don Benito?
A: The road cycling routes around Don Benito primarily feature paved surfaces, making them suitable for all skill levels. You'll traverse diverse landscapes, including scenic rural areas, the Sierra de Ortiga with its cork oaks and granite domes, and routes around the Embalse de Orellana. While most are paved, some more difficult segments might include unpaved sections.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Don Benito?
A: Yes, Don Benito has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992933348/don-benito-villanueva-de-la-serena-circular-by-the-population-of-la-haba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito - Villanueva de la Serena — circular by the population of La Haba&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 40.9 km (25.4 miles) trail that takes less than two hours to complete, passing through pleasant rural Extremaduran landscapes. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito loop from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 26.5 km (16.5 miles) ride.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation around Don Benito?
A: While many routes are moderate, some offer more significant elevation gains. The region's varied terrain, including areas like the Sierra de Ortiga, provides opportunities for more demanding rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito – La Haba loop from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 83.1 km (51.6 miles) route with nearly 700 meters of elevation gain, offering a good challenge.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Don Benito?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Don Benito are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994503704/medellin-castle-loop-from-don-benito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medellín Castle Loop from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt;, a 69.3 km (43.0 miles) moderate ride, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rena (Badajoz) – Don Benito loop from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 34.3 km (21.3 miles) route.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Don Benito often integrate historical sites and natural beauty. You can explore rural Extremaduran landscapes, the Sierra de Ortiga with its cork oaks and granite domes, and ride near the Embalse de Orellana. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theater and Castle of Medellín&lt;/a&gt;, which you can view on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994503704/medellin-castle-loop-from-don-benito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medellín Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Other attractions in the wider area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Don Benito?
A: Don Benito, located in Extremadura, Spain, generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season. Winters are generally mild, allowing for cycling, though checking local weather forecasts is always advisable.

Q: Are there options for parking near the cycling routes in Don Benito?
A: Don Benito, as a town with an active cycling community and a history of hosting cycling events, typically offers accessible parking options. Many routes start directly from the town, making it convenient to park in municipal areas or near the starting points of popular loops. Specific parking details may vary by route, but general town parking is usually available.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Don Benito?
A: The road cycling routes around Don Benito are highly regarded by the komoot community, with over 600 road cyclists having explored the area using komoot. Users frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of historical sites with natural beauty, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels. The region's well-established cycling culture and extensive network of routes contribute to a positive experience.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in the Extremadura region?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads and established routes in the Extremadura region around Don Benito. The area is open for cyclists to explore its rural landscapes and historical paths freely. However, always respect private property and any local regulations that might apply to specific natural parks or protected areas, though these are less common for road cycling.

Q: How long do the road cycling routes around Don Benito typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road cycling routes around Don Benito varies significantly depending on the distance and your pace. Easy routes, like the 40.9 km (25.4 miles) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992933348/don-benito-villanueva-de-la-serena-circular-by-the-population-of-la-haba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito - Villanueva de la Serena — circular by the population of La Haba&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in under 2 hours. Moderate routes, such as the 83.1 km (51.6 miles) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Don Benito – La Haba loop from Don Benito&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 3 hours and 48 minutes. There are options for shorter rides of about an hour and longer excursions of several hours.
tips:
Medellín Castle is one of Extremadura's major historical attractions, perched atop a hill overlooking the Guadiana River and the town of Medellín (Badajoz).
Origin: A 9th-century Muslim fortress, built upon Roman ruins.
After the Reconquista, it fell into Christian hands and was renovated in the 13th and 15th centuries.
It held great strategic importance on the border with Portugal and in the defense of the Guadiana River.
A key location in the Battle of Medellín (1809) during the Peninsular War.

The Bridge of Philip IV in Medellín (Badajoz, Extremadura) is one of the most emblematic historical bridges over the Guadiana River.
Built in the 17th century during the reign of Philip IV, it was constructed to improve communication between the town of Medellín and the surrounding region.
It replaced earlier structures and became a strategic point for trade and transit in the area.

Don Benito is a city that surprises both visitors and residents. The historic center, with its Plaza de España, the Church of Santiago Apóstol, and Renaissance buildings, attracts visitors.
Outstanding museums such as the Ethnographic Museum (rated 4.6/5) and the Automobile Museum are well-received.
Urban parks like Tierno Galván, the Palmeral, and Las Albercas offer green spaces appreciated by tourists and locals alike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Castillo de Medellín circular route from Don Benito?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is approximately 69.3 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 51 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full circular route starting and ending in Don Benito.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is considered moderate. While much of it is on well-paved surfaces, it covers a distance of nearly 70 km with an elevation gain of about 210 meters, requiring a good fitness level. There's also a notable climb over cobbled ground to reach the castle.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: Cyclists can expect mostly well-paved road surfaces throughout the route. However, be prepared for a significant and memorable climb over cobbled ground as you approach the Castillo de Medellín.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Castillo de Medellín itself, offering spectacular views. You'll also pass by the Medellín Roman Bridge and cycle through the town of Don Benito.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the climb to the Castillo de Medellín provides spectacular vistas, including expansive views of the surrounding 'Vegas Altas' region and the Guadiana river.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The broader Extremadura region is known for its flower-filled meadows, suggesting spring (March to May) could be an excellent time for pleasant weather and scenic beauty. Autumn (September to November) would also offer cooler temperatures ideal for cycling.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car in Don Benito to start the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for cyclists in Don Benito are not detailed, as a starting and ending point, you should be able to find public parking within the town of Don Benito. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: This specific route is rated as moderate and requires a good fitness level due to its length and elevation gain. An easier, shorter variation (around 22 km with 100m elevation) exists in the area, which might be more suitable for beginners or families, but this particular route is for more experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several official trails for short sections. These include parts of the Camino Mozárabe, Camino Romano - Etapa 3, Camino Natural del Guadiana, and the Cañada Real Leonesa Occidental.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Castillo de Medellín cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces in Spain, it's not typically suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists on a 69 km road ride. If you plan to visit the castle grounds, check their specific pet policies.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits to cycle this public road route. There are no entrance fees for the cycling trail itself. If you plan to enter the Castillo de Medellín, check for any admission fees at the site.
